title: feat: Implement autoupdate endpoint in libpod REST API
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

This PR implements a new libpod REST API endpoint providing the autoupdate functionality, as it is included in the podman CLI. It accepts the same options as the CLI version. The end goal of this is to allow applications, such as cockpit-podman, to perform container updates in a similar manner to what is possible with the CLI.

This PR also modifies the swagger-check script. Since the endpoint is `/libpod/autoupdate`, the linter expected the response to be named `autoupdateListResponse`. The name `autoupdateResponse` made more sense in my mind so I added an exception for this in the swagger-check script. If this is not intended, I can remove it and use the suggested name instead.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Added auto update endpoint to the REST API
```

comments.body: Regarding tests, this will require running a container from a systemd unit and performing tests on the api. Is this currently supported in the test system, and if so, can someone with more knowledge of the project point me in the right direction?

The simplest way to test this feature is by adding an auto-update capability to the remote client and then enabling the [test for it.](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/system/255-auto-update.bats)

[Remote client code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/auto-update.go)

I agree with podman-remote we can reuse the existing tests, and podman-remote used the remote APi so that is tested as well.

There are api only tests in test/apiv2 but there is no good way to run systemd unit containers there at the same time so I would definitely prefer the podman-remote option.

These changes implement autoupdate support for podman-remote. Using the existing test for this is a good idea I think. Looking through the test file it interacts with systemd, but wouldn't these commands be executed on the client side?

If not, is it enough to just remove the `skip_if_remote` statement in the setup?

> If not, is it enough to just remove the skip_if_remote statement in the setup?

yes removing the skip_if_remote will make the test run via podman-remote. And yeah systenctl command are still local but we only test these tests locally so remote and server are the same system so that should work fine.

One note though the `podman-auto-update --authfile` will need the skip_if_remote now though as the local registry only work with the local podman.

> > If not, is it enough to just remove the skip_if_remote statement in the setup?
> 
> yes removing the skip_if_remote will make the test run via podman-remote. And yeah systenctl command are still local but we only test these tests locally so remote and server are the same system so that should work fine.

Okay, that's good.

> One note though the `podman-auto-update --authfile` will need the skip_if_remote now though as the local registry only work with the local podman.

Not exactly sure what you mean here. I took some inspiration from [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/906f87b985594b77b20d9deada351c0cc73f6e61/pkg/bindings/manifests/manifests.go#L100) to add the registry auth to the request. I see now that I don't handle this in the api endpoint like [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/906f87b985594b77b20d9deada351c0cc73f6e61/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/manifests.go#L182). I assume I'll need to add that in and would that not make the test case work too?

> > One note though the `podman-auto-update --authfile` will need the skip_if_remote now though as the local registry only work with the local podman.
> 
> Not exactly sure what you mean here. I took some inspiration from [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/906f87b985594b77b20d9deada351c0cc73f6e61/pkg/bindings/manifests/manifests.go#L100) to add the registry auth to the request. I see now that I don't handle this in the api endpoint like [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/906f87b985594b77b20d9deada351c0cc73f6e61/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/manifests.go#L182). I assume I'll need to add that in and would that not make the test case work too?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9f7c6c38216460c21735ca9746cc488db0de77a8/test/system/255-auto-update.bats#L648

start_registry will only ever work locally, so this test needs skip_if_remote for just that one test, i.e. compare
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9f7c6c38216460c21735ca9746cc488db0de77a8/test/system/156-pull-policy.bats#L5-L7

Apologies for the delay, I had a few busy weeks. Last three commits do the following:
 - Use authfiles from the client machine, like is done for other remote commands.
     - Previously it would take the path from the client and attempt to find a file on the server, now it actually takes the authfile on the client and uses the MakeXRegistryAuthHeader function to send it to the server.
 - Report pull errors back to the client
     - Previously the API would only return the auto update reports, causing pull errors to not be shown on the client. 
 - Enable the auto update tests for podman-remote, skipping for registries as suggested.

@Luap99 could you help me out with these last failing test cases? It seems to be failing on something related to the local cache registry, from the [containers-cached.conf](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/registries-cached.conf) file. It is rewriting the location but it is somehow ignoring the insecure flag and the test cases fail on it being a http repository. 

I do see some references like `skip_if_remote "containers.conf settings not set for remote connections"` in some test cases (like [150-login.bats](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/116e4588c4b7c6f93ec1f20354bd9e89cc14fed3/test/system/150-login.bats#L370)). Could it be that the cached registry is not supported for podman-remote?
Oh and please squash the commits into one once you are done.

That seems to fix those cases. Thanks a lot!

These two failing test cases seem unrelated to this PR. Is that right?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *StefanNienhuis* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
machines tests are quite flaky I restarted them they are definitely unrelated to your changes.
Okay. I rebased on main. Not really familiar with the PR process here, but looking through the document linked by the bot it seems I need a LGTM from someone?
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Now all systemd units are failing to start. Can't reproduce it when running tests locally though.

We require two reviews so the second person is merging the PR normally.

Looking at the tests the big rework from https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24601 likely broke it, I need to figure out how to deal with that

Rebased on main, finally have some time again. I think the failing test cases are caused by the use of remote in the generated systemd files.

E.g.
```
ExecStart=/home/stefan/podman/bin/podman \
	--url tcp://localhost:8888 run \
    <other args...>
```

I think the issue has something to do with sdnotify not being propagated through remote, so systemd is failing the units. But the inclusion of the remote url in the systemd units is strange in itself, since they are meant to be placed on the remote machine (as is also indicated in a warning when running generate systemd).

I'm not really sure where to fix this, it feels like a bug in `generate systemd`, but that's also deprecated. @Luap99 do you have any suggestions?

@StefanNienhuis sorry for the long delay, yeah looking at it I guess we cannot really run test/system/255-auto-update.bats with remote without way to much other changes.

So I guess let's keep it the way there were and maybe instead you can use a test case in test/apiv2 that just does a quick one shot auto update without a running container in systemd. That would at least allow to ensure the API can be called.
@StefanNienhuis Any updates on this?
@StefanNienhuis Please rebase onto main. We merged a new CI configuration a few minutes ago because Cirrus CI is no longer active.
Nice timing :) should be good now.
It looks like you haven't rebased on the latest main. Please check remotes, etc...
Not sure what happened, should be good now.
